name: OfficeCLI
tagline: An Office suite built for agents, not humans
description: A from-scratch document engine in a single self-contained binary: reads, edits, and creates .docx, .xlsx, and .pptx, renders to HTML or PNG, evaluates Excel formulas, and runs template merges — no Microsoft Office installation anywhere.
why: Inverts the Office-automation stack. Instead of scripting Word through COM or fighting format libraries, agents get a headless, deterministic document engine that behaves like a tool, because it is one.
useCases: Letting coding agents produce real Office documents headlessly
audience: Agent builders, automation engineers, document-pipeline developers

setup.requirements: Download one cross-platform binary (.NET-based); invoke from a shell or agent tool call

limitations: Format fidelity against real Office has edge cases; complex layouts and macros are out of scope. Validate output before it goes anywhere business-critical.
take: Agent-native tooling is becoming its own software category. This is what it looks like when someone rebuilds a 40-year-old product assuming the user is a machine.
